one_line_takeaway: Proposes 'relearning convergence delay,' a convergence-speed-based metric for how resistant an unlearned model is to a relearning attack that recovers supposedly forgotten data, plus the IEU unlearning method (gradient ascent on the forgetting set + noise-based weight re-initialization) that empirically achieves a smaller utility gap to retrained reference models than FT/RL/SCRUB/SALUN baselines on TinyImageNet classification and NSFW-content removal from Stable Diffusion, but the authors' own theory and experiments show the approach weakens under Adam-based relearning and has not been tested outside vision.
key_finding: On TinyImageNet/ResNet-50 (Table 1, 30% random forgetting), IEU w/GA achieves Avg. Gap 0.024 and IEU w/Noisy also 0.024, versus Retraining's reference 0.000, FT's 0.050, RL's 0.059, SCRUB's 0.054, and SALUN's 0.115 -- i.e., IEU variants have roughly 2-4x smaller average gap to the retrained reference model than the strongest baseline. At 50% random forgetting, IEU w/Noisy reaches 0.026 versus FT 0.066, RL 0.068, SCRUB 0.076, SALUN 0.118. On class-wise forgetting (Table 2, 30%/50%), IEU variants achieve Avg. Gap of 0.012-0.014 versus FT's 0.012-0.013 (comparable), RL's 0.021-0.025, SCRUB's 0.033-0.037, and SALUN's 0.077-0.095 (SALUN notably worse). The paper reports combining GA+Noisy does not improve over either component alone. On Stable Diffusion unlearning (Table 3), ESD w/Noisy attains the best combination for the ESD backbone (Nudity score 0.224 vs ESD baseline's 0.413, FID 0.510 vs 0.880), while SALUN w/GA+Noisy achieves the lowest Nudity score in the SALUN setting (0.250 vs SALUN baseline's 0.500) and the lowest FID overall (0.350). RCD_Adam values for the generation setting decrease as more mitigation components are added (e.g., ESD 538.022 to ESD w/GA+Noisy 517.166), which the authors note in their own limitations section is a weaker/less discriminating trend than the classification results because gradient-descent-derived RCD theory does not transfer cleanly to Adam-based relearning.
core_contribution: The paper introduces two things as its own contribution: (1) 'relearning convergence delay' (RCD), a new metric for machine unlearning that quantifies how quickly an unlearned model relearns forgotten data via fine-tuning convergence speed (framed as an infinite integral over the gap between the relearning trajectory's loss and the optimal loss on the forgetting set, with a finite-K approximation and a proven O(e^-K) approximation error), together with theoretical upper bounds on RCD under Gradient Descent tied to the model's condition number on the forgetting set; and (2) the Influence Eliminating Unlearning (IEU) framework, which unlearns a model by (a) minimizing loss on the retaining set, (b) explicitly maximizing loss on the forgetting set via Gradient Ascent with a smaller step-size c*eta_r, and (c) an 'Iterative Re-initialization Process' that stochastically interpolates model weights toward random Kaiming-initialized noise (Noisy Regularization) to raise the condition number on the forgetting set and thereby slow relearning. The authors also give a convergence guarantee (exponential rate O(e^-t)) for IEU's error on the retaining set as a function of hyperparameters alpha and c.
threat_model: The paper's own framing is a privacy/compliance threat: forgotten data may still be recoverable from an 'unlearned' model, either passively (residual weight influence) or actively via a 'relearning attack' in which an adversary or auditor fine-tunes the released unlearned model on (partial) forgetting-set data and observes how fast it reconverges to good performance on that data -- fast reconvergence indicates the model retained latent knowledge of the supposedly forgotten data, defeating GDPR/AI-Act 'right to be forgotten' guarantees. The paper does not model a specific adversary capability set (e.g., query budget, white-box vs black-box); it treats the relearning attacker as having white-box access to fine-tune the model and adopts the existing MIA (membership inference attack, measured via attack success rate) as a secondary privacy leakage metric from prior work, not a paper contribution.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) the theoretical RCD bounds and the IEU convergence guarantee are derived under Gradient Descent and mu-strongly-convex/beta-smooth loss assumptions, which do not hold for the Adam optimizer commonly used in modern training -- and the paper's own Stable Diffusion experiments show the RCD/relearning-resistance framework is 'less effective when the Adam optimizer is used for relearning'; (2) IEU's scalability and adaptability beyond vision tasks (e.g., language, graph domains) is explicitly flagged as unsolved and unexplored; (3) combining GA and Noisy components did not yield further improvement over either alone in classification experiments, an unexplained result the authors do not fully account for; (4) IEU shows 'slightly worse MIA scores' than baselines despite better accuracy/Avg.-Gap trade-offs, a tension the paper notes but does not resolve; (5) the authors explicitly flag that fairness and security implications of unlearning 'require thorough investigation' as future work, i.e., they do not claim to have addressed those implications in this paper.

quantitative_results: TinyImageNet/ResNet-50, 30% random forgetting: Avg. Gap -- Retraining 0.000, FT 0.050, RL 0.059, SCRUB 0.054, SALUN 0.115, IEU w/GA 0.024, IEU w/Noisy 0.024, IEU w/GA+Noisy 0.027
TinyImageNet/ResNet-50, 50% random forgetting: Avg. Gap -- FT 0.066, RL 0.068, SCRUB 0.076, SALUN 0.118, IEU w/GA 0.031, IEU w/Noisy 0.026, IEU w/GA+Noisy 0.028
TinyImageNet/ResNet-50, 30% class-wise forgetting: Avg. Gap -- FT 0.012, RL 0.025, SCRUB 0.037, SALUN 0.095, IEU w/GA 0.013, IEU w/Noisy 0.012, IEU w/GA+Noisy 0.014
TinyImageNet/ResNet-50, 50% class-wise forgetting: Avg. Gap -- FT 0.013, RL 0.021, SCRUB 0.033, SALUN 0.077, IEU w/GA 0.013, IEU w/Noisy 0.009, IEU w/GA+Noisy 0.012

Stable Diffusion NSFW unlearning (Table 3): ESD baseline FID 0.880 / Nudity 0.413 / RCD_Adam 538.022; ESD w/GA FID 0.515 / Nudity 0.415 / RCD_Adam 526.653; ESD w/Noisy FID 0.510 / Nudity 0.224 / RCD_Adam 524.057; ESD w/GA+Noisy FID 0.428 / Nudity 0.330 / RCD_Adam 517.166; SALUN baseline FID 1.938 / Nudity 0.500 / RCD_Adam 530.431; SALUN w/GA FID 0.627 / Nudity 0.337 / RCD_Adam 523.846; SALUN w/Noisy FID 0.742 / Nudity 0.272 / RCD_Adam 525.421; SALUN w/GA+Noisy FID 0.350 / Nudity 0.250 / RCD_Adam 506.544
baselines_compared: Retraining (as the gold-standard reference for Avg. Gap)
